title: brachychiton australis subsp. incanus
intro: Brachychiton australis subsp. incanus, commonly known as the rusty-bronze kurrajong, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Sterculiaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and tannins. The health benefits of the rusty-bronze kurrajong include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically to the skin or ingested to promote overall well-being.
